articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Free Wi-Fi hotspots for Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe is set to mark its 46th Independence Anniversary with the nationwide rollout of free public Wi-Fi hotspots, a government initiative aimed at expanding digital access and fostering innovation among young people. Speaking at the third Zimbabwe Annual Telecommunications Conference and Exhibition in Harare, the Minister of ICT, Postal and Courier Services, Tatenda Mavetera, said […]

more.title: Gweru Cracks Down on Littering with US$30 Spot Fine
more.description: Gweru Cracks Down on Littering with US$30 Spot Fine Gweru City Council has introduced a US$30 spot fine for littering as authorities step up efforts to improve cleanliness and safeguard public health. The move comes after a high-level waste management meeting involving Government departments, environmental agencies, law enforcement and civic groups, amid growing concern over [...] The post Gweru Cracks Down on Littering with US$30 Spot Fine appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: A brief history of Zimbabwean politics through the eyes of Nicolo Machiavelli
more.description: What if Zimbabwe’s political story is not tragic because it went wrong, but because it went exactly as power demands? For decades, Zimbabweans have narrated their politics as a sequence of betrayals, missed opportunities and strongmen who refused to let go.
